---
title: "What's New"
subtitle: "Codex Port Release History"
---

# v4.2.0 — Workflow Parity Restoration {#v42}

*Released 2026-04-11*

This release restores the missing worker-critic execution contract in the active Codex surface instead of leaving it stranded in legacy reference material.

## Runtime Activation

- The repo-local `clo-author` plugin now ships with a local marketplace entry for default-install support
- Added `plugins/clo-author/references/runtime-activation.md`
- Quick start now includes an activation smoke test before relying on natural-language skill routing
- Added a machine-level fallback installer for `~/.agents/skills/clo-author` when the live Codex session does not load the repo-local bundle

## Active Enforcement Layer

- Promoted critic gating, phase blocking, and three-round escalation into the active references
- Expanded the active research-journal schema with round number, approval state, and next actor
- Rebuilt the active skill files so they again specify dispatch, review, and save semantics explicitly

# v4.1.0 — Canonical Upstream Realignment and Enforcement Parity {#v41}

*Released 2026-04-11*

This release re-aligns the live Codex surfaces with the canonical Clo-Author upstream at `hugosantanna/clo-author` and ports the major `v4.1.0` enforcement features into the Codex-native architecture.

## Canonical Upstream

- Replaced stale downstream-fork links in the guide, README, and plugin metadata
- Clarified that this repository is the Codex-native port while `hugosantanna/clo-author` is the canonical upstream
- Added an explicit sync model so future maintenance compares against the right repository

## Content Invariants

- Added `plugins/clo-author/references/content-invariants.md`
- Reviewers now have a stable invariant list for paper, code, and talk checks
- Hard requirements such as `biblatex` + `biber`, relative paths, and caption ownership are now explicitly numbered

## Pre-Flight Reports

- `strategize` now requires a Pre-Strategy Report before proposing an identification design
- `analyze` now requires a Pre-Code Report before writing scripts
- Both workflows now make missing inputs explicit instead of silently assuming context

## Decision Records and Linting

- Added `templates/decision-record.md` and the `quality_reports/decisions/` convention
- Added a portable grep-based linter at `plugins/clo-author/hooks/lint-scripts.sh`
- The `review` and `tools` workflows now surface linting as an explicit mechanical pre-check

---

# v4.0.0 — Codex-Native Architecture {#v4}

*Released 2026-04-10*

This was the first Codex-native port release: `AGENTS.md` became the live control plane, the repo-local plugin replaced slash-command orchestration, and the guide/docs were rewritten around Codex workflows.
